meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no design skills (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no design skills. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing UI for Freelancers: No Design Skills Needed
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI. Learn how to generate high-converting SaaS pricing tables using Antigravity and Claude Code. A pro-level guide for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: Eliminate design bottlenecks by using Antigravity to generate semantic, responsive UI components and Claude Code to inject them into your stack. By focusing on data-driven pricing structures and modular CSS grid layouts, you can deploy professional, high-converting pricing tiers in minutes without manual design iteration.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the backend logic, the database schema, and the client's vision—yet you spend four hours tweaking hex codes and margins on a pricing table that still looks like a sophomore CS project. Your client doesn't care about your struggle; they care about conversion rates and time-to-market.

We are shifting the paradigm. By leveraging Antigravity for structural generation and Claude Code as an autonomous pair-programmer, you stop 'designing' and start engineering. This workflow treats UI as a deterministic output, allowing you to ship enterprise-grade pricing modules while your competitors are still debating font weights.

steps.title: Define the Semantic Structure
steps.description: Before generating code, define the pricing tiers and feature sets as a JSON configuration to ensure consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for a 3-tier SaaS pricing model including: Basic, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for monthly/yearly price, feature arrays, and a boolean for 'is_recommended'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a config.json file in the /data directory with the provided pricing structure.'

steps.title: Generate the Component Skeleton
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the React component using Tailwind CSS for rapid styling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React Pricing component using Tailwind CSS. Use a grid layout, include hover states for the 'Pro' card, and ensure all buttons have accessible focus states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Generate PricingComponent.tsx in /components using the provided Antigravity snippet.'

steps.title: Inject Dynamic Data
steps.description: Map the JSON data into the component to ensure the UI is data-driven and easily updated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing component to map over the imported config.json data instead of hardcoded strings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor PricingComponent.tsx to import data from @/data/config.json and map through tiers.'

steps.title: Implement Conversion Logic
steps.description: Add the toggle functionality between monthly and yearly billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a React state toggle for Monthly/Yearly billing. Update the pricing display dynamically based on the selected state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add a toggle state in PricingComponent.tsx to multiply prices by 10 for yearly and display a -20% discount badge.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Audit
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform an accessibility and responsiveness audit on the final component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform an accessibility audit on the pricing component. Check for contrast ratios, ARIA labels, and tab index order.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Check PricingComponent.tsx for accessibility compliance and ensure responsiveness on mobile breakpoints.'

pro_tips: Always define your design system tokens in a global CSS file before generating components to ensure the AI uses your brand colors.
Use 'ghost' buttons for lower-tier plans to create a natural visual hierarchy that guides the eye toward your primary 'Pro' offering.
Inject micro-copy into your pricing tiers; AI models often default to generic features, but specific benefits increase conversion by 30%.
Never manually write CSS; ask the AI to generate Tailwind classes based on a reference image of a high-converting competitor.
Use Claude Code to generate Playwright tests for your pricing toggle to ensure the UI doesn't break during future refactors.
content.next_steps: Integrate your new pricing component with a payment gateway like Stripe using the same AI-driven scaffolding approach to maintain UI consistency across your checkout flow.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a top-tier agency. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
